role_title: Senior AI Product Manager, Finance Agents

level_signals: 3–6 years of direct experience in Finance industry
Compensation range $205K–$257K base in SF/NY/Seattle consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3–6 years of direct experience in the Finance industry in one or more of: investment banking, private equity, trading, asset or portfolio management, strategic finance, corp dev, or a fintech product role, with real depth in how the work gets done.
AI-Forward Thinking: Quick to adopt AI solutions to streamline your day-to-day workflows and an ability to understand and articulate how it's transforming your industry.
A builder's mindset: excited by ambiguity and motivated to create new products from the ground up.
Bias for action: Comfort wearing multiple hats and operating in fast-moving environments.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Product or customer-facing experience: A track record of owning outcomes, shaping roadmaps, or working closely with technical stakeholders (formal PM experience is a plus but not required if domain depth is strong).
ML Intuition & Technical Fluency: Enough intuition around how model training and evaluation works and what a Reinforcement Learning environment looks like. A software background is a plus.
A software background is a plus.

scope.surface: Finance vertical within Agents Data & Reinforcement Learning Environments team — RL environments and data-as-a-product strategy for financial AI agents
scope.business_model: B2B data and AI infrastructure (frontier labs and enterprise customers)
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality and coverage of Finance RL environments
Adoption by frontier AI labs of Finance training products
Benchmark launches and brand presence in Finance AI
core_responsibilities: Own the Finance AI roadmap & data strategy
Set product direction for the Finance agents training stack and data strategy
Build partnerships with research teams at frontier labs
Design and scale Finance-specific RL environments (financial models, investment memos, forecasts, dashboards, data rooms)
Collaborate cross-functionally across research, operations, and customer interactions

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Clear product ownership area with well-defined scope (Finance vertical RL environments)
Role description explicitly states formal PM experience is not required if domain depth is strong — lowers barrier for finance practitioners
Strong entrepreneurial mandate — building new product lines from the ground up
Exposure to frontier AI lab researchers and high-profile customers
red_flags: 3–6 year finance industry experience framed as 'Ideally, You'd Have' (nice-to-have header) but is listed first and described in very specific terms — ambiguity on whether this is hard or soft
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
Role is onsite in SF, NY, or Seattle — no remote option mentioned
Wide comp band ($205K–$257K) spans multiple career levels per posting language, making seniority level harder to pin down
90-day waiting period policy before reconsidering candidates for the same role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + title + years range)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from city-specific pay transparency statement and no remote mention)
ambiguities: All requirements are listed under 'Ideally, You'd Have' which typically signals nice-to-haves, but the 3–6 years finance industry experience appears to be a true hard requirement given the core premise of the role. It has been classified as hard with a note here.
The comp range language states it 'may be inclusive of several career levels at Scale' — seniority level and exact tier cannot be confirmed from JD alone.
Location policy is described only via pay transparency for SF, NY, Seattle — remote eligibility is not explicitly addressed (classified as onsite based on available evidence).
